---
title: "008 — Hidden-Markov fuel regimes: the energy-era calendar"
date: 2026-06-12
---

## Question

Fuel shares drift, spike and reverse — but underneath, do states occupy a small
number of discrete **energy regimes**, with rare, sticky switches between them?
If yes, the regime calendar (which state was in which era, when) is a cleaner
unit of analysis than raw shares, and the switch years should line up with
policy shocks. (Background: *Hidden-Markov Fuel Regimes* card in the
[guide](guide.qmd).)

## Setup

```{python}
import duckdb
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from complexity_lab.config import settings
from complexity_lab.complexity.regimes import fit_fuel_regimes, transition_years

con = duckdb.connect(str(settings.db_path), read_only=True)
panel = con.execute(
    "SELECT state_code, year, petrol_share, diesel_share, cng_share, ev_share, hybrid_share "
    "FROM panel_state_year WHERE year < (SELECT MAX(year) FROM panel_state_year) "
    "ORDER BY state_code, year"
).df()
res = fit_fuel_regimes(panel)
res["selection"].round(1)
```

## The regimes the data chose

```{python}
res["regime_means"].round(3)
```

```{python}
tm = res["transition_matrix"].round(3)
px.imshow(tm, text_auto=".2f", color_continuous_scale="Blues",
          title="Yearly regime transition probabilities (rows → columns)",
          labels={"x": "to", "y": "from"})
```

## The regime calendar

```{python}
cal = res["calendar"]
order = (cal.groupby("state_code")["regime"].mean().sort_values().index.tolist())
fig = px.imshow(
    cal.pivot(index="state_code", columns="year", values="regime").loc[order],
    aspect="auto", color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
    title="Regime calendar — each state's decoded era by year",
)
fig.update_layout(height=700, coloraxis_showscale=False)
fig
```

## When do states switch?

```{python}
trans = transition_years(cal)
px.histogram(trans, x="year", color="to_regime",
             title="Regime-switch years across states (align with BS6 2020, EV policies 2021-23)")
```

## Finding

```{python}
diag = res["transition_matrix"].to_numpy().diagonal()
print(f"Regimes: {list(res['regime_means']['regime_label'])}")
print(f"Persistence (diagonal): {[round(float(d),3) for d in diag]}")
print(f"{len(trans)} switches across {res['n_states_fit']} states; "
      f"modal switch year: {int(trans['year'].mode().iloc[0])}")
```

The BIC-selected model describes India's fuel transition as a small set of
sticky eras rather than a continuum: persistence sits near 1, switches are
rare, cluster in 2020–2022 (BS6 + COVID + the EV policy wave), and reverse
flows back toward the fossil-heavy regime are essentially absent — the
transition behaves like a **ratchet**. The publishable extension is a Cox
survival model on time-to-switch with income, infrastructure and fuel-price
covariates (blueprint Project B, step 5).
